base_context.description: The next run will be this leader/base config plus ONLY the JSON changes returned by the advisor.

expected_direction: increase net profit or PnL per exposure hour without worsening MAE
expected_effect: Should expose whether VALID_1DTE_ONLY contains a separable pullback/reclaim regime while preserving the existing breakout_long_1dte edge. Desired outcome is flat-or-better combined 1DTE PnL, improved 1DTE average/worst MAE or exposure efficiency, and no damage to VALID_0DTE performance. Main tradeoff is router cannibalization: if pullback_long_1dte simply relabels profitable breakout trades or reduces 1DTE PnL below the current $1315.3 slice, reject.
label: one-dte-pullback-companion-attribution
rationale: Use a new-profile-config router split rather than another global veto or repeated call-wall/charm-wall keyset. run_058 has clean data-state attribution: VALID_1DTE_ONLY contributes 20 trades, $1315.3 PnL, no slow trades, but worst MAE is still -386.75 and median hold is longer than the best 0DTE pullback/charm edges. Direct 1DTE tightening has been risky, so the better experiment is attribution: add the dormant pullback_long_1dte companion to the one-DTE router and measure whether it separates reclaim behavior from breakout continuation. This is one logical family and does not allow option-confirmed 0DTE profiles to trade on missing/stale/thin 0DTE data.
